test:backend.notes: 144 passed, 7 errors. The 7 errors are in test_admin_users.py where fixtures use real database (AsyncSessionLocal) instead of test database. Fixed SQLAlchemy relationship ambiguity in community/models.py.

test:frontend.notes: 2 passed, 29 failed. All failures due to React version mismatch: 'A React Element from an older version of React was rendered.' This is a test infrastructure issue with Next.js + vitest bundling, not a code bug. Fixed vi import issue (was importing from 'jest' instead of 'vitest').

lint:frontend.notes: Passed with only warnings (unused-vars, no-img-element, no-explicit-any).

typecheck:frontend.notes: TypeScript type check passed with no errors.

reviewsSummary.notes: Did not spawn review subagents because validators still fail. Per skill procedure: 'Do not proceed to feature review' when validators fail.

blockingIssues.description: Most subscription module code remains UNTRACKED in git. Files not tracked: apps/api/internal/subscriptions/models.py, repository.py, router.py, schemas.py, service.py, and frontend files (pricing/page.tsx, PremiumGate.tsx, subscriptions.ts). Only __init__.py is tracked.
Server-side premium endpoint gating NOT implemented. The require_premium_access dependency may exist but is not applied to any API endpoints. Premium content is not protected by 403 responses.
No tests created for subscription module. No test_subscriptions.py exists in apps/api/tests/. The backend-worker skill requires TDD but no subscription tests were written.
Expected behavior 'Renewal reminder: notification sent 3 days before expiry' is NOT implemented. No background task or notification logic exists for subscription renewal reminders.

fetchCurrentUser() returns User type that does NOT include 'role' field. The User interface only has {sub, email, name, picture}. Moderator UI checks (currentUserRole) will never work because role is never fetched.
blockingIssues.location: apps/web/src/lib/auth.ts - User interface and transformUser function

blockingIssues.description: Report button on discussion detail page has no onClick handler. Users cannot report inappropriate content.
Frontend test infrastructure broken: React version mismatch causes all component tests to fail. vi.mock issue fixed but React dual-copy problem persists. Tests cannot run.

Backend admin tests use real database connection instead of test database. admin_user_token fixture uses AsyncSessionLocal() which connects to real postgres, causing password auth failures in test environment.

appliedUpdates.description: Fixed SQLAlchemy relationship ambiguity by adding explicit foreign_keys to Discussion.replies, DiscussionReply.discussion, DiscussionUpvote.discussion, and DiscussionUpvote.reply relationships.
appliedUpdates.issue: AmbiguousForeignKeysError: Can't determine join between 'discussions' and 'discussion_replies'

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add explicit requirement that all new feature code must be committed to git BEFORE marking feature as complete. Untracked files should be treated as incomplete. Workers must run `git add` and `git commit` before declaring feature done.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: subscription-module has 8 out of 9 files untracked despite being marked 'completed'

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify TDD requirement - reviewers must verify test files exist and pass, not just that curl/agent-browser verification was done.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: subscription-module marked complete but has no test file despite TDD skill requirement

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add requirement: API client functions must be verified to exist before importing in components.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: community-module imports fetchCurrentUser but User type doesn't include role field needed for moderator checks

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add test infrastructure requirement: all test fixtures must use test database overrides, never real database connections.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: test_admin_users.py admin_user_token fixture uses AsyncSessionLocal() connecting to real postgres
